reasons: Barats wants to stay inside fights while maintaining Big Guy stacks, and Estes helps extend that frontline uptime with healing.
Estes supports the grouped teamfight pattern where Barats anchors the fight and nearby teammates continue fighting around him.

proof.summary: Barats stays in the middle of the fight with Big Guy stacks, while Estes keeps him and nearby teammates alive through extended teamfight healing.
worksBestWhen: Barats enters with Big Guy stacks already active.
Estes stays close enough to maintain healing links without being the first target.
The team fights around Barats instead of scattering away from Estes.
failureCases: The enemy applies anti-heal before Barats commits.
Barats loses stacks before the objective fight starts.
Estes is burst or zoned before his healing window matters.
Barats dives too far away from Estes's healing range.

reasons: Angela can attach to Barats during his frontline commit, adding shield and sustain while he holds space.
Barats's durable body gives Angela a stable host during extended fights.

proof.summary: Barats absorbs pressure as a durable frontliner, while Angela adds shield and support during his commit window.
worksBestWhen: Angela attaches before Barats is forced too low.
Barats enters with Big Guy stacks already active.
failureCases: Barats loses stacks before Angela commits.
Angela attaches after Barats is already chain-controlled.
The enemy ignores Barats and dives the backline instead.

reasons: Floryn can help Barats survive longer while he contests objectives or holds frontline.
Her healing is useful when Barats is already committed and cannot easily reset.

proof.summary: Barats stays active in the center of the fight, while Floryn adds healing support to extend his frontline uptime.
worksBestWhen: Barats has stacks before the fight.
Floryn saves healing for the real commit, not small poke trades.
failureCases: Barats is displaced away from the team.
Floryn is picked off before Barats can start the fight.

reasons: Barats can suppress and displace a target with Detona's Welcome, giving Pharsa a clear ranged burst target.
Pharsa can follow Barats's pickoff without needing to stand inside the frontline.

proof.summary: Barats isolates or throws a target, while Pharsa follows from range with burst damage.
worksBestWhen: Barats throws the target toward team damage.
Pharsa waits for the target-control window before using burst.
failureCases: Pharsa is too far to follow.
The target survives and Barats is left deep without support.

reasons: Barats gives Cecilion a strong frontline body to cast behind.
Barats can control enemy movement and create predictable target windows for Cecilion's ranged magic damage.

proof.summary: Barats holds the frontline and controls space, while Cecilion follows from range with scaling magic damage.
worksBestWhen: Barats keeps enemies in front of Cecilion's casting angle.
Cecilion keeps distance and does not walk into Barats's melee zone.
failureCases: Barats throws the target away from Cecilion's range.
Cecilion is dived before Barats can peel.
Barats loses stacks before the fight begins.
